normal uterus and ovaries.base_explanation: This means the uterus and ovaries appear normal in size and structure on imaging.

uterine fibroid.base_explanation: A uterine fibroid is a common, non-cancerous growth of the uterus muscle.
uterine fibroid.follow_up_note: The impact of fibroids depends on their size, number, and location.

simple ovarian cyst.base_explanation: A simple ovarian cyst is a fluid-filled sac in the ovary and is commonly seen during reproductive years.
simple ovarian cyst.follow_up_note: Many simple cysts resolve on their own without treatment.

complex ovarian cyst.base_explanation: A complex ovarian cyst contains both fluid and solid components, which may require closer evaluation.
complex ovarian cyst.follow_up_note: Doctors consider size, appearance, and symptoms when deciding next steps.

polycystic ovaries.base_explanation: Polycystic ovaries describe the appearance of multiple small follicles in the ovaries on imaging.
polycystic ovaries.follow_up_note: This imaging finding alone does not diagnose a hormonal condition.

endometrial thickening.base_explanation: Endometrial thickening means the lining of the uterus appears thicker than usual.
endometrial thickening.follow_up_note: Normal thickness varies with age and menstrual cycle phase.

endometrial thinning.base_explanation: Endometrial thinning means the uterine lining appears thinner than average.
endometrial thinning.follow_up_note: This may be normal depending on age or hormonal status.

adenomyosis.base_explanation: Adenomyosis is a condition where uterine tissue grows into the muscle wall of the uterus.
adenomyosis.follow_up_note: Symptoms vary, and imaging findings are interpreted with clinical history.

pelvic free fluid.base_explanation: Pelvic free fluid refers to a small amount of fluid seen in the pelvic cavity.
pelvic free fluid.follow_up_note: This can be a normal finding depending on timing in the menstrual cycle.

ovarian follicle.base_explanation: An ovarian follicle is a normal structure involved in the menstrual cycle.
ovarian follicle.follow_up_note: This is a physiological and expected finding.
